summary: Packaged desktop compute-node startup hung or failed before Registered: yes across an original llama_cpp discovery hang, a child import watchdog timeout, and a subprocess facade early exit.
impact: Windows packaged CUDA operators could fail before relay registration, and the shared packaged import/facade seam also risked macOS Metal parity.
fix: Reused desktop runtime setup probe diagnostics, normalized extended Windows paths in subprocess envs, made the subprocess facade import/model-init path report JSON errors, captured child stdout/stderr/exit diagnostics, and kept lifecycle tests gated on registered=true.
lessons: Packaged e2e must prove registered=true for the full desktop + relay lifecycle and simulate native-runtime child failure shapes. Any child native-extension runtime path must share the successful probe bootstrap contract and surface child stderr/stdout/exit code on early exit.
